questions.text: I find that I process conversations primarily through their informational content.
I hold the view that maintaining a measured, rational perspective during emotionally intense moments is a strength that serves the greater good.
I hold the view that a person who takes care of themselves is in the best position to live a good life.
I approach conflict by focusing on outcomes rather than on the emotions involved.
I prefer to find a path everyone can accept rather than force a choice.
I hold the view that my emotional life is rich in its own way, even if it's not dramatic.
I believe straightforward communication is more productive than nuanced signaling.
I am attuned to when someone feels embarrassed even if they hide it well.
I routinely surround myself with people and environments that elevate my personal trajectory.
I believe that the moral concern I feel when encountering suffering is an accurate perception of something real and important.
I sometimes feel overwhelmed by the sheer intensity of being alive.
I tend to carve out my own lane rather than share one.
I create templates and standard procedures so I don't have to reinvent the wheel.
I often have multiple projects going at once and move between them as my interest dictates.
I experience disappointment as a clear acknowledgment rather than a gut punch.
I prefer that priorities be established through group discussion.
I tend to deal with maintenance issues only when something actually breaks.
I hold the view that my job is not done until everyone else's needs are met.
